tasks.label: Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads)
A fun option available right now (casual game stream / hobby session, solo, repeatable)

memory.summary: I have a free afternoon to complete a 3-hour scholarship application that could significantly reduce my costs, while also having an optional fun activity available for enjoyment.

Starting the scholarship application now creates a buffer for potential errors, reducing Thursday stress. Since fun activities are flexible and not time-locked, I can enjoy them later without pressure.
I spent 2 hours on the scholarship application but made little progress, leaving half my free block unused and increasing my stress despite previous plans.
I wasted a free afternoon on the scholarship application instead of the available fun option, increasing my stress and leaving little progress.
I made more progress than expected in two hours, but my stress remains high due to unused free time and prior wasted efforts.
After 1.5 hours, my progress increased to 0.66 toward the 3-hour goal, yet my stress remains high due to prior wasted time and remaining sleep debt.
After 1.5 hours, progress reached 0.72 of the 3-hour goal, but high stress and 2 hours sleep debt persist despite recent gains.
I completed 30 minutes of the scholarship application, raising progress to 78% with only two hours left. Despite this improvement, my high stress and sleep debt remain unchanged.
Progress on the scholarship application improved to 84%, but my high stress and two hours of sleep debt remain unchanged despite recent gains.
Progress on the scholarship application improved to 90%, nearing completion, yet my high stress and two hours of sleep debt remain unchanged.
Worked 1.5 hours to reach 96% completion on the scholarship application, but stress and sleep debt remain high.
Worked 0.5h to reach 97% on the scholarship application, but stress and sleep debt remain high despite nearing completion.

action.name: work_on

trajectory.observation: Worked 2h on Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads): progress 0.05 -> 0.23. (you realize) Starting the application now leaves slack to absorb friction (a missing transcript upload, an essay rewrite) before Thursday; each hour invested early removes ~1.5h of crunch and lowers Thursday stress. The award is still attainable either way as long as the form is submitted on time — this is an adjustable-cost optimization, not an irreversible loss. (you realize) The fun option recurs later in the week and any time after the application is in; nothing about it is one-time or socially time-locked, so taking it later costs nothing.
